content.full_text:
July 21, 1976


Honorable Morris J. Stephan (Ret.)
Santa Barbara County
Department No. Sm-2
P.O. Box 1299
Santa Maria, California

Dear Judge Stephan:

     Thank you for your recent inquiry concerning your filing
obligations under the Political Reform Act as a result of recent
legislation which requires that judges of courts of record dis-
close certain financial interests.

     Only persons who are currently serving or have been appointed
or resigned as judges of courts of record since May, 1976 and pos-
sibly retired judges who accept assignments, will be required to
file financial disclosure reports.

     As a judge who retired prior to the effective date of the
lerislation and who does not accept court assignments you will
have no obligation to file a Statement of Economic Interest.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw

bcc: Jeanne P.


POU-SP-4


[illegible]-confidential
[illegible] Classified Information
[illegible]


CHAMBERS OF THE
SUPERIOR COURT
MORRIS J. STEPHAN
JUDGE

Retired

COURT HOUSE

TELEPHONE
WALNUT 2-7709

MAIL ADDRESS
P.O. BOX 1068
91454

P.O. Box 1299

SANTA BARBARA COUNTY
DEPARTMENT NO. SM-2
VETERANS' MEMORIAL BUILDING
RM. 5 TUNNEL
SANTA MARIA, CALIFORNIA

July 13, 1976

The Technical Assistance/Administration
Section of the Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

I do not have available the new legislation concerning
judges filing their financial and property assets. I do not
know whether it applies to retired judges who are receiving
only their retired pay. Since I am drawing no judicial income
other than retired pay, will you kindly advise whether I will
have to comply with the legislation. If so, send me the
necessary forms. Thanking you.

Respectfully yours,

Morris J. Stephan
Morris J. Stephan
Judge of Superior Court
Retired

sjb
